title: senecio gypsicola
intro: Senecio gypsicola, also known as the 'Gypsum Senecio', is a rare succulent plant species native to Western Australia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain toxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids, although it has been used traditionally in folk medicine for its purported benefits. The plant has been associated with potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, but its use is not recommended due to the presence of toxic compounds. Herbal preparations of Senecio gypsicola are not commonly used or recommended, and its use should be approached with caution due to the potential risks associated with its toxic alkaloids.
